name: Athena RPC: upload queue (listUploadQueue, cancelUpload)
description: Add two openpilot athenad RPC methods that let the server inspect and manipulate the device's upload queue. listUploadQueue returns the list of in-flight/pending UploadItem dicts the device is trying to send; cancelUpload accepts a single upload_id string (or a list of IDs) and removes them from the queue. Reference: openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py lines 449-467 (../../openpilot/system/athena/athenad.py). This is a prerequisite for the upload-queue-page frontend feature.
acceptance_criteria: internal/ws/methods.go defines an UploadItem struct with JSON tags matching athenad's UploadItemDict (id, path, url, headers, priority, retry_count, created_at, current, progress, allow_cellular)
CallListUploadQueue(caller, client) ([]UploadItem, error) is added, calling the listUploadQueue RPC and unmarshaling the array result

CallCancelUpload(caller, client, ids []string) (map[string]interface{}, error) is added; if len(ids)==1 it sends a single-string param, otherwise sends the list (matches athenad's str|list[str] signature)
RegisterDefaultHandlers installs device-side stubs that return an empty queue / a success ack
internal/ws/methods_test.go covers both Call* functions, including the single-id vs list-of-ids branches for cancelUpload
go vet ./... and go build ./... pass cleanly
